SECTION A
Name: 				Dr. Pritam Singh
Present appointment: 		Professor Grade S1 Department of Accounting, Finance and Economics

SECTION B: TEACHING, LEARNING AND ASSESSMENT

In addition to my excellent teaching record validated by student evaluation and peer review, I have also made substantial contribution to pedagogical research as evidenced below through my publications, editorial board membership, conference papers and keynote addresses.
Book
1999	Equal Opportunities in the Curriculum, Oxford Centre for Staff and Learning Development, Oxford Brookes University, co-edited with Martyn Pearl.
Editorial Board Membership
2007-present	International Journal of Pluralism and Economics Education
2004-15	Brookes eJournal of Learning and Teaching
Conference Papers
2007	‘Researching International Development: Contributions to Curriculum Development and External Recognition’, OBU Business School Annual Conference (June)

2005  	‘Gender Bias in Curriculum and Research in Higher Education: Reflections with special reference to the area of development studies’, Fourth European Conference on Gender Equality in Higher Education, OBU (August 31- September 3)
2005	Presentation on ‘Addressing Euro-centricity in learning and teaching’ to a Visiting Party from University of Utrecht, OBU (February 24)
Keynote Speaker/Distinguished Visitor

2010	Keynote address on ‘From Pakhi Khurd to Oxford: Reflections on the Role of Teachers’ at the Punjab State-level Conference of College Principals organised by the Punjab State Education College (Patiala, Punjab, India; January 16)

2010	Distinguished Visitor and a special address on ‘Innate abilities and choosing a future career: resisting market pressures’, to the school students and teachers of the Khadoor Sahib Higher Secondary School (Amritsar, India; Jan 13)

2010	Keynote address on ‘Teaching methodogies: some lessons from the comparative experience of teaching in India and the UK’ at the International workshop on ‘Teaching Methodologies in India and Abroad’, Baba Farid Group of Institutions (Bathinda, Punjab; January 4)
SECTION C: RESEARCH

My reputation as a scholar of international standing has been built through a sustained publication record of very high quality and active participation in national and international networks of the academic community. Since joining OBU in 1988, I have published 5 books (2 single authored; 3 co-authored), 34 refereed journal articles, 21 book chapters, 3 review articles, 60+ non-refereed articles, 25 book reviews, numerous newspapers articles, and delivered 80 conference papers. I have been invited to present 30 keynote addresses and 44 talks. I have organised 4 international conferences and chaired/convened 35 panels at national and international conferences.

The University chose to submit my research output for Research Excellence Framework submission 2013 to the Politics and International Relations Unit of Assessment. My publications for the REF 2021 are equally strong for submission to the Business and Management UoA, or to the Politics and International Relations UoA.

I have played an active role as a research leader, nationally and internationally, through my professional associations and editorial responsibilities as well as, within OBU, through mentoring younger colleagues in doctoral supervision teams.  I lead the Environmental and Development Economics research cluster and member of the Grants Panel of the Faculty of Business.

My research has significantly influenced mine and other colleagues’ teaching. My books and articles are on the reading lists of a number of modules in the Faculty of Business; the module evaluation reports describe in detail the links between my research and teaching, and a number of students’ dissertations at all levels (PhD, PG and UG) have been inspired by my research.

Academic Reviewer and Referee
I have reviewed book proposals to Oxford University Press, Routledge, Pluto and Pearson, and of new journal proposals to Taylor and Francis.
I have reviewed research bids to the British Academy and ESRC.

I have given Subject Expert advice (as a South Asian Development Studies scholar) to Flemish Interuniversity Council- University Development Cooperation in Belgium regarding a research grant on March 7, 2008.

I have been a referee for the journals - Third World Quarterly, Economic and Political Weekly, Journal of Punjab Studies, International Journal of Green Economics, Contemporary South Asia, South Asian History and Culture, International Journal of Pluralism and Economics Education, Science Journal of Economics, Millennial Asia and Journal of South Asian Development.
I have reviewed conference papers as Member Review Committee, Association for Heterodox Economics, for annual conferences between 2012 and 2016.
I was a Member of the Academic Review Board, 4th International Conference on Institutional and Technological Environment for Microfinance Financial Inclusion, Paris, April 11-12, 2013.
PUBLICATIONS
Books (published)

2015	Punjabi Identity in a Global Context, Oxford University Press, 2nd edition (co-edited with S Thandi, equal contribution of the editors). First edition 1999. About a dozen reviews of the book have appeared in the leading journals of South Asian studies.

2010	Economy, Culture and  Human Rights: Turbulence in Punjab, India and Beyond, Three Essays Collective, Delhi (sole author). Reviewed in leadng international journals including Contemporary South Asia, Journal of Punjab Studies, Pacific Affairs and Panjab University Research Journal (Social Sciences). A feature article on the book was carried by The Tribune (the largest circulating English daily in North West India) on April 10, 2011 and a review was also carried by The Tribune on May 26, 2013.  A 32 minute TV interview on the book has been watched world wide: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AZKdYO0Sz_Q

2008 	Federalism, Nationalism and Development: India and the Punjab Economy, Routledge, London/New York (sole author). A paperback edition and also a Special Indian reprint was brought out in 2009. Reviewed in Contemporary South Asia, Economic and Political Weekly (EPW), Journal of Asian Studies, Journal of South Asia Development and The Tribune. The EPW review remarked: ‘The book is one of those rare academic publications which have the potential to make history’.

Non-Refereed Articles and Book Reviews

I have published over 60 articles and several letters in newspapers and news magazines in the UK and South Asia and 25 book reviews. In the last 2 years (August 2014 - September 2016) I have written over 20 opinion/comment articles on global development issues in The Tribune, a leading English daily in India. With my specialisation in development studies, I communicate my research widely by writing newspaper/magazine articles and book reviews in journals; this contributes significantly to extending the social impact of my research.
Translations of my work in other languages
2017	My book Federalism, Nationalism and Development (Routledge, 2008) has been translated into Punjabi and is due for publication in 2017.

2014	My book chapter (co-author Lok Nath Bhusal), ‘The rise and fall of the welfare state in the UK’ has been translated into Polish and is included in the Polish edition of Krzysztof Frysztacki (ed.) Cracovian Studies in Social Work, Jagiellonian University Press, Cracow, Poland, 2014.

Joint bid participation

As a member of the Executive of the British Association of South Asian Studies, I was involved in the successful bid by BASAS led by Prof Graham Chapman and Prof Peter Rob for a grant (£37,000) from the British Academy Learned Societies Programme in 2007.
Past bids

My bid for ESRC funding (£100,000) three years ago for a project on ecology and development in India’s Punjab region progressed to the last stage with a grade of 7.5 (threshold for success that year was 8.5). By modifying this project, I prepared a bid for the Leverhulme Research Fellowship in 2012 but was unsuccessful. I am working with our Research Team to prepare a bid for other potential sources of funding(e.g. Nuffield Foundation, Institute for New Economics Thinking and UKIERI (the UK India Education and Research Initiative).
Internal Funding
I won a CRF2 grant of £18,979 in May 2014 for a project on the impact of India-China rivalry on Nepal’s development.
I was a co-bidder with Prof Peter Edge for internal funding in 2009-10. The project “Shariah and Legal Globalisation” received a £17,100 award from the Central University Research Fund.
I won an internal research grant (FoB) of £5,000 to work on Centre-Punjab Financial Relations Under Two Policy Regimes. Project period (July 2012-Dec 2013, extended to April 2015).

External Examination of Doctoral Dissertations

I have externally examined 10 PhD dissertations: one each in India and Ireland, three in the UK (Lancaster, Huddersfield and Coventry), one in the USA (Rutgers) and four in Pakistan. All were in the area of Development Studies and/or South Asian Studies.

Other public engagment activities and contributions to voluntary sector

I have been advisor to the Oxford Sikh community for over 15 years in the community’s campaign to secure a permanent place of worship in Oxford. I have laised with Oxford’s MPs, city councillors; given interviews to TV channels, radio stations and newpapers,  This campaign succeeded eventually  in 2012 with the community  building a Gurdwara (Sikh temple).

As a convenor of the Oxford South Asia Forum, I have played a central role in organising discussions on South Asian issues, screening of meaningful Indian films, and have thrice organised fund raising dinners to help fund the rehabiliation of the victims of natural disasters in Pakistan, Sri Lanka and Burma.

I have also worked with Asylum Welcome, Campaign to Close Campsfield Detention Centre and the Medical Foundation for the Treatement of Victims of Torture in their campaigns to protect refugees and immigrants.
SECTION E: ACADEMIC LEADERSHIP
Examples from within OBU:
I currently lead the Environmental and Development Economics research cluster in my department.
Field Chair of the Economics Department (2000-03) and Research Leader of the Department (2003-04).
Member of the Corporate Identity Group, which interviewed and selected the consultants who coined the name and logo for the new University in 1992.
Convener of the Ethnic Minority Staff Forum for the full duration of the existence of the Forum.

On behalf of the Equal Opportunity Action Group (EOAG), I was co-organiser of a very successful conference at OBU on equal opportunities in the curriculum that eventually resulted in the publication of a book Equal Opportunities in the Curriculum (1999).

As a representative of the EOAG, I sat on many selection and recruitment panels (as an observer) for promotions to Principal Lectureship in the university with the objective of embedding EOs in the university’s staff selection and promotion process.

Member of the Environment Forum, from its inception. I have taken an active part in all the sustainability and fair trade campaigns launched by the university that culminated in the University being designated as the first Fair Trade University in the UK.
I collaborated with Prof Peter Edge in a bid for Central Research Fund and participated in a very impressive international conference on Shariah and Human Rights.

Seven years ago, I led the development of a masters programme (MSc in International Management and International Relations) to be taught jointly by the Business School and the School of Social Sciences and Law and was appointed Programme Director of the new MSc. I directed the programme during its first foundational three years (2008-11).
SECTION F: ENTERPRISE AND KNOWLEDGE TRANSFER
I have been a member of:

the Business Section of the Amnesty International that promotes the importance of embedding human rights monitoring in the conduct of business by international organisations. My 2010 book Economy, Culture and Human Rights has further developed their work in this direction.

the Core Committee for the joint project by South Asia Forum for Human Rights and Canada’s International Development Research Centre on  A Human Rights and Peace Audit Exercise on Partitions as a Method to Resolve Ethno-Nationalist Conflicts in South Asia.
I have provided consultancy to:
The Immigration and Refugee Control Board of Canada, regarding the history, culture and politics of the Punjab to aid their decision-making on immigration/asylum policies in 2014 and 2015.

Amnesty International (AI), regarding human rights practices and trends in India. AI invited me to give lectures in Derby, Edinburgh, Henley, London, Oxford during its India campaign. I also translated the Universal Declaration of Human Rights for AI in Punjabi.
West Midlands Arts Council, regarding aspects of Punjab and Sikh culture and heritage.

House of Commons’ All Party Parliamentry Committee on Punjabis in Britain. I provided background historical and political knowledge required for policy decision-making. Acknowledgement of my work has been made in parliamentary speeches by John McDonnell MP, recorded in Hansard.
Canada’s International Development Research Centre and South Asia Forum for Human Rights (Hong Kong and Kathmandu), regarding a range of human rights issues.
BBC World Service and BBC Oxford, who have sought my advice several times on South Asian, Indian and Sikh affairs, in addition to formally interviewing me for TV and Radio broadcast.
